posts.title: New Jersey won't turn over voter roll records, but they want to know every person that purchased a Glock in the past 10 years.

link_metadata.description: Last year, the New Jersey Attorney General’s Office filed a lawsuit against Glock, Inc. under the state’s public nuisance law. This week, in connection with that lawsuit, FFLs across the state started receiving subpoenas demanding production of customer records regarding the lawful sale of all Glock pistols to New Jersey residents for the last ten years.
link_metadata.title: NRA-ILA | New Jersey: Attorney General Sends Subpoenas to Statewide FFLs Seeking Customer Records
